title: faba vulgaris subsp. minor
intro: Faba vulgaris subsp. minor, also known as vetch or hairy vetch, is a sub-species of the faba bean plant, belonging to the legume family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Faba vulgaris subsp. minor include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract, and can also be used as a food ingredient or added to animal feed due to its nitrogen-fixing properties.
